sections.paragraph:
fut. יִזְרַח-

(1) to rise, used of the sun, Gen 32:32 Exo 22:2 2Sa 23:4 Psa 104:22, etc.; also applied to light, Isa 58:10 to the glory of God, 60:1 , Isa 60:2 Deu 33:2. (It properly means to scatter rays, comp. the kindred words זָרָה, זָרַר. This root is variously changed in the cognate languages; hence in Arabic and Ethiopic شرق ሠረቀ፡, in Aramean דְּנַח, ܕܢܚܰ.)

(2) It is figuratively applied-(a) to leprosy breaking out in the skin,-(b) in the derivatives also to a fœtus breaking forth from the womb (see זֶרַח and Gen 38:30), and-(c) to a plant springing up, i.q. פָּרַח, see אֶזְרָח .

Derivatives, אֶזְרָח, מִזְרָח, pr.n. יִזְרַחְיָה, and the words immediately following.
